title: Africa Unpacked: How the continent is navigating an oil supply crisis
author: Sanele Mjikane
text:
Africa Unpacked | Oxford Economics Africa

Africa Unpacked: How the continent is navigating an oil supply crisis

Our Africa Unpacked sessions give our lead economists and senior political analysts the opportunity to engage with your team and provide our view on the most important economic and political themes affecting the continent.

These engaging sessions allow us to discuss what our team of researchers are seeing, reading, and thinking, and how this could have an impact on your business and influence your decision-making.

In our latest sessions, we take stock of how the continent has navigated the repercussions of the conflict in the Middle East. In the truncated presentation preview, we briefly discuss:

Our global commodity price assumptions for Brent crude and fertiliser.

Show how our GDP growth forecasts have been adjusted since February for several major African markets.

Discuss the most recent inflation figures and what some governments can do to placate price pressures.

Fill in the form to access the presentation deck.

About Oxford Economics

Oxford Economics is the world’s leading independent economic advisory firm. Covering over 200 countries, 100 industrial sectors and 8,000 cities and regions, we support major consumer-facing businesses in interpreting the macroeconomy through a commercial lens, translating macro trends into clear, actionable implications for demand, cost and growth.
